name: 奥兰多·迪·拉索
brief: 拉索出生于今比利时蒙斯，年少时因嗓音出众三次被人试图拐走。他游历意大利多年，在罗马、那不勒斯和米兰积累了丰富的音乐经验，后定居慕尼黑，在巴伐利亚公爵宫廷担任乐长长达三十余年。拉索是文艺复兴时期最多产的作曲家之一，创作了超过2000部作品，涵盖弥撒曲、经文歌、牧歌、法语歌曲和德语歌曲等几乎所有体裁。
briefEn: Lasso was born in Mons in present-day Belgium and was reportedly kidnapped three times as a boy on account of his beautiful singing voice. After years of travel in Italy — gaining rich musical experience in Rome, Naples, and Milan — he settled in Munich, where he served as Kapellmeister at the Bavarian ducal court for over thirty years. One of the most prolific composers of the Renaissance, Lasso produced more than 2,000 works spanning nearly every genre: masses, motets, madrigals, French chansons, and German Lieder.
contribution: 拉索将欧洲各国的音乐风格融会贯通——意大利牧歌的抒情、法国歌曲的精致、德国利德的质朴、佛兰德复调的严密，在他的笔下浑然一体。他被视为文艺复兴复调音乐的集大成者，与帕莱斯特里纳齐名，但风格更加多元和戏剧化。他的《忏悔诗篇》将文字绘画法（Word Painting）推向了新的高度。
contributionEn: Lasso synthesized the musical traditions of all Europe — the lyricism of the Italian madrigal, the elegance of the French chanson, the directness of the German Lied, and the rigor of Franco-Flemish counterpoint — forging them into a unified personal language. He is considered the great synthesizer of Renaissance polyphony, equal in stature to Palestrina yet more versatile and dramatic. His Penitential Psalms elevated the art of word painting to unprecedented heights.
style: 与帕莱斯特里纳的沉静内敛不同，拉索的音乐充满戏剧性与情感张力。他是文字绘画法的大师——音乐精确地描绘歌词中的每一个意象：叹息、哭泣、飞翔、坠落都能在音符中找到对应。他的和声大胆而富于色彩，节奏灵活多变，世俗作品中常带有幽默和讽刺的语气。
styleEn: In contrast to Palestrina's serene restraint, Lasso's music brims with dramatic intensity and emotional force. He was a supreme master of word painting — his music mirrors every image in the text with uncanny precision: sighs, weeping, soaring, and falling all find vivid musical equivalents. His harmonies are bold and richly colored, his rhythms supple and varied, and his secular works often carry a tone of wit and irony.

famousScores.name: 《忏悔诗篇》 Psalmi Davidis poenitentiales
famousScores.desc: 七首忏悔诗篇的宏大套曲，文艺复兴宗教音乐的巅峰
famousScores.descEn: A monumental cycle of seven penitential psalms, the pinnacle of Renaissance sacred music
famousScores.detail:
《大卫忏悔诗篇》约作于1560年，是拉索为巴伐利亚公爵阿尔布雷希特五世创作的私人宗教作品集，包含诗篇中的七首「忏悔诗篇」（第6、32、38、51、102、130、143篇）以及一首结语经文歌。公爵将这部作品保存为私密的宫廷收藏，并未立即公开出版，直到拉索去世多年后的1584年才正式印行——由此可见公爵对这部作品的珍视，将其视为私家珍藏而非广为传播的商品。

这部作品附有手绘插图的豪华羊皮纸手稿，每首诗篇配以精美的细密画，是文艺复兴时代艺术与音乐完美结合的例证。在音乐上，拉索将文字绘画法推向了新的极致：诗篇中的每一情感转折都在音乐中找到了精确的对应，哀泣、恳求、悔恨、感恩在音乐中如此真实，使人忘记这是五百年前的作品。

《大卫忏悔诗篇》是文艺复兴宗教音乐最具表现力的作品之一，其情感深度和戏剧张力远超同时代任何宗教音乐，甚至预示了巴洛克时代音乐修辞学的到来。19世纪的舒曼和勃拉姆斯都曾表达对拉索这部作品的高度敬意，认为它是真正「走在时代前面」的杰作。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Psalmi Davidis Poenitentiales was composed around 1560 as a private devotional gift for Duke Albrecht V of Bavaria, setting the seven biblical Penitential Psalms (Psalms 6, 32, 38, 51, 102, 130, and 143) along with a concluding motet. The Duke treasured it as a personal possession and did not commission its publication; it only appeared in print in 1584, years after Lasso's death — a clear sign that the Duke regarded it as a private treasure rather than a commodity for circulation.

The work survives in a magnificent illuminated manuscript on parchment, each psalm accompanied by miniature paintings, making it one of the supreme examples of Renaissance art and music fused into a single unified object. Musically, Lasso pushed word painting to new extremes: every emotional shift in the psalms — weeping, supplication, repentance, gratitude — finds a precise musical equivalent, with an immediacy that makes the five-century distance seem to vanish entirely.

The Psalmi Davidis stands as one of the most expressive religious works of the Renaissance, its dramatic intensity and emotional range exceeding almost anything written by a contemporary. Its foreshadowing of Baroque musical rhetoric and textual expressivity led nineteenth-century admirers including Schumann and Brahms to regard it as a work genuinely ahead of its time — a verdict that has only grown more convincing with the passage of years.

famousScores.name: 《女巫预言》 Prophetiae Sibyllarum
famousScores.desc: 12首极端半音化经文歌，和声的大胆程度预示了杰苏阿尔多乃至20世纪音乐
famousScores.descEn: A cycle of 12 motets in an extremely chromatic idiom — harmonies so bold they anticipate Gesualdo and even the 20th century
famousScores.detail:
《女巫预言》是拉索最具实验性的作品，由12首经文歌组成，以古代女先知（西比拉）的拉丁文预言诗为词，这些诗被认为预言了基督的降临。这组作品约创作于1550年代，和声风格极为激进：拉索使用了大量半音进行、增音程和不寻常的和弦并置，产生了一种游离、神秘、仿佛超越调性的音响效果，令同时代人感到困惑和震惊。

这组作品的引言经文歌开头有一句著名的话：「用色彩丰富的和声歌唱多声部的旋律」（Carmina chromatico quae audis modulata tenore），直接宣示了拉索对半音化风格的有意追求。这种极端半音主义在16世纪只有那不勒斯的杰苏阿尔多（Carlo Gesualdo）能与之相提并论，而拉索的版本更早，影响也更广。

在音乐史上，《女巫预言》是一个令人着迷的异数——它在数百年间几乎被遗忘，直到20世纪的古乐复兴中才重新被发现，并立即被认定为一部超时代的杰作。今天，音乐学家将这部作品视为从文艺复兴「调式体系」向巴洛克「调性体系」过渡的最重要预示之一，它的大胆程度甚至令现代听众感到惊讶。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Prophetiae Sibyllarum is Lasso's most experimental work — a cycle of twelve motets setting Latin verse attributed to the ancient Sibyls, whose prophecies were believed to foretell the coming of Christ. Composed probably in the 1550s, the harmonic language of these pieces is startlingly radical: Lasso employs dense chromatic progressions, augmented intervals, and unprecedented juxtapositions of chords, creating a floating, mysterious sound world that seems to dissolve the boundaries of any given mode.

The preface motet boldly announces Lasso's intention with the words 'Carmina chromatico quae audis modulata tenore' ('Songs you hear set in chromatic melody') — a direct declaration that the extreme chromaticism is deliberate and purposeful. In the sixteenth century only the Neapolitan Carlo Gesualdo approached a similar level of harmonic adventurousness, but Lasso's cycle predates Gesualdo's madrigals and influenced a far wider musical world.

In music history, the Prophetiae Sibyllarum stands as a fascinating anomaly — largely forgotten for centuries, it was rediscovered in the twentieth-century early music revival and immediately recognized as a work of visionary power. Music historians now view it as one of the most important harbingers of the transition from Renaissance modal thinking to Baroque tonality, its audacity astonishing even to modern ears schooled in the dissonances of the twentieth century.

famousScores.name: 《回声》 O la, o che bon eccho
famousScores.nameEn: O la, o che bon eccho
famousScores.desc: 八声部双合唱团的回声效果作品，趣味横生
famousScores.descEn: A delightful eight-voice double-choir piece exploiting the spatial effect of musical echoes
famousScores.detail:
《回声》是拉索最著名的幽默作品之一，为两个四声部合唱团（共八个声部）而写，利用两组歌手的空间分离制造「回声」的音响效果。第一组歌手演唱一句旋律，第二组则紧接着以较弱的力度重复其最后几个音节，模仿自然中的回声现象。这种做法在威尼斯的分合唱传统中已有先例，但拉索将其与幽默的文字游戏结合在一起，创造出独特的喜剧效果。

作品的歌词充满双关和机智，回声的重复不仅是音响效果，更制造出幽默的对话——原句的一部分被「回声」重复时，往往产生出新的含义，令人忍俊不禁。拉索以这种半哲学半幽默的方式探讨了「回声」这一自然现象：它是语言的镜子，还是自然的嘲笑？

这首作品展示了拉索作为喜剧音乐家的天赋，证明了文艺复兴复调的技巧同样可以服务于纯粹的音乐趣味。它在16世纪的贵族宴会和宫廷娱乐中广受欢迎，至今仍是古乐合唱团的保留节目，以其轻盈幽默的气质令听众喜笑颜开。在巴洛克时代，空间音响和回声效果进一步发展，威尼斯的乔瓦尼·加布里埃利（Giovanni Gabrieli）将分合唱写法推向了更加宏大的境界。
famousScores.detailEn:
O la, o che bon eccho is one of Lasso's most celebrated comic pieces, written for two four-voice choirs (eight voices in total) placed in spatial separation to create the acoustic illusion of a natural echo. The first ensemble sings a melodic phrase, and the second immediately answers by repeating its final syllables at a softer dynamic, mimicking the phenomenon of sound reflecting off a distant surface. While divided-choir writing had precedents in the Venetian tradition, Lasso combined the spatial effect with clever wordplay to create something uniquely comic.

The text is filled with puns and wit: when the second choir 'echoes' the end of a phrase, the repeated syllables often yield a new and amusing meaning, as if Nature were playing a joke on the singer. Lasso treats the echo as both a natural marvel and a philosophical riddle — a mirror of language, or perhaps Nature's own ironic commentary. The piece is a masterclass in musical comedy that uses formal techniques to generate genuine laughter.

The work reveals Lasso's exceptional gifts as a comic composer, proving that the most rigorous Renaissance polyphony could also serve pure musical delight. It was enormously popular at noble banquets and court entertainments in the sixteenth century, and it remains a favorite of early music choirs today. In the Baroque era, the spatial and echo effects it exploited were developed on a far grander scale by Giovanni Gabrieli, whose divided-choir works for St. Mark's Basilica in Venice are direct descendants of this tradition.

famousScores.name: 《泪涌如泉》 Lagrime di San Pietro
famousScores.desc: 临终绝笔，20首精神牧歌套曲，晚年最深沉的杰作
famousScores.descEn: His final testament — a cycle of 20 spiritual madrigals and the most profoundly expressive work of his late years
famousScores.detail:
《圣彼得的泪水》是拉索的最后作品，写于1594年他去世前数周——据说他的学生为他保管羽毛笔，以免垂死的大师在精神崩溃之际毁掉这部心血之作。作品由20首精神牧歌组成，以16世纪意大利诗人路易吉·坦西洛（Luigi Tansillo）描写使徒彼得背叛耶稣后悔恨泪水的诗篇为词，最后附以一首经文歌《我悲伤》（Vide homo）。整组作品以七个声部演唱，是拉索有史以来创作的声部最多的声乐套曲。

拉索在生命的最后时刻，似乎将自己与悔罪的彼得完全合而为一。这20首牧歌的情感弧线从极度的悔恨与绝望开始，逐渐走向隐约的平静与接受，仿佛是一个垂死者的灵魂旅程。和声语言极其大胆，有些段落的不协和音之尖锐和痛苦令人震惊——拉索在生命的最后关头再次证明，他是那个时代最勇于突破的声音。

《圣彼得的泪水》被誉为文艺复兴时代最具深度的声乐杰作之一，它在精神上与蒙特威尔第的第五册牧歌相连，是从文艺复兴走向巴洛克表现主义的关键作品。20世纪的指挥家们重新发掘这部作品后，它迅速成为古乐复兴的代表性曲目，被誉为拉索留给后人最珍贵的音乐遗言。
famousScores.detailEn:
Lagrime di San Pietro ('The Tears of Saint Peter') was Lasso's final work, composed in the weeks before his death in 1594. According to contemporary accounts, his pupils kept the pen from the dying master to prevent him from destroying the manuscript in a moment of mental anguish. The cycle sets twenty spiritual madrigals by the Italian poet Luigi Tansillo, describing the Apostle Peter's tears of repentance after his betrayal of Christ, followed by a concluding motet, Vide homo ('Behold, O Man'). Scored for seven voices, it is the largest-voiced vocal cycle Lasso ever wrote.

In his final months, Lasso seems to have identified completely with the repentant Peter. The emotional arc of the twenty madrigals moves from extreme grief and despair toward a fragile, hard-won acceptance — the journey of a dying soul. The harmonic language is extraordinarily bold: some passages deploy dissonances so raw and painful that they can still shock a modern listener. At the threshold of death, Lasso once again proved himself the most daring musical voice of his age.

Lagrime di San Pietro is now widely regarded as one of the most profound vocal works of the Renaissance. Spiritually it connects to Monteverdi's Fifth Book of Madrigals; historically it stands at the very border between Renaissance and Baroque expressivism. Rediscovered in the twentieth century, it quickly became a landmark of the early music revival, hailed as Lasso's most precious final gift to posterity — a testament written not in words but in pure musical tears.

famousScores.name: 《Matona mia cara》
famousScores.desc: 幽默的意大利牧歌，模仿德国士兵蹩脚的意大利语
famousScores.descEn: A humorous Italian villanella parodying a German soldier's clumsy attempts at Italian
famousScores.detail:
《亲爱的玛托纳》是拉索众多世俗小品中最广为人知的一首，属于意大利「维拉内拉」（Villanella）风格——这是一种起源于那不勒斯的轻松、带有民间色彩的声乐体裁，通常以幽默甚至有些粗俗的歌词见长。这首歌描绘了一名德国士兵用蹩脚的意大利语向窗户里的意大利女人献殷勤，其方言错误、语法颠倒和笨拙的韵律都被拉索精确地「翻译」成了音乐上的不协调感，令人忍俊不禁。

拉索本人在慕尼黑服务于巴伐利亚宫廷，身边接触到的正是这样的德国军人。他用意大利人的眼光，温和地嘲笑了德国人学意大利语的笨拙，同时又以真挚的幽默让被嘲笑者也忍不住发笑。这种跨文化的幽默感，正是拉索作为泛欧洲作曲家最迷人的性格特征之一——他能在德、意、法三种音乐语言之间自如切换，也能用音乐理解不同民族的性格与幽默。

这首小品证明了伟大的艺术家不一定非得端着架子。拉索用最轻巧的形式写出了最有人情味的音乐，它的幽默跨越了五百年，至今还能让音乐会的观众哈哈大笑。这种将高超技巧服务于纯粹欢乐的能力，是拉索区别于他那个时代许多严肃复调大师的独特之处。
famousScores.detailEn:
Matona mia cara is the most widely known of Lasso's many secular pieces, written in the style of the Neapolitan villanella — a light, folk-tinged vocal genre typically featuring comic or even bawdy texts. The song depicts a German soldier attempting to woo an Italian woman at her window in comically broken Italian: his grammatical errors, inverted syntax, and awkward rhymes are translated by Lasso into musical incongruities and mock-folk rhythms that make the humor immediately audible, even to listeners who do not understand the words.

Lasso himself spent most of his career at the Bavarian court in Munich, surrounded by precisely such German soldiers. Viewing them with an Italian eye, he gently mocked their linguistic fumbles while ensuring that the humor was warm enough to make even the Germans laugh along. This cross-cultural comic sensibility was one of Lasso's most endearing qualities as a pan-European musician: he could switch between German, Italian, and French musical idioms with equal ease, and he understood the temperament and humor of each nation from the inside.

The piece proves that great artists need not always be solemn. Lasso deployed his supreme skill in the lightest of forms to produce the most genuinely human of music — a humor that has survived five centuries completely intact, still producing laughter in concert halls today. This ability to place virtuosity entirely at the service of joy distinguishes Lasso from many of his more earnest polyphonic contemporaries and makes him a uniquely approachable figure across the centuries.

famousScores.name: 《某日苏珊娜》 Susanne un jour
famousScores.desc: 16世纪流传最广的法语香颂之一，以圣经故事苏珊娜为题，拉索五声部版本被公认为最出色
famousScores.descEn: One of the most widely disseminated French chansons of the 16th century, setting the biblical Susanna story — Lasso's five-voice version is the most celebrated
famousScores.detail:
《某日苏珊娜》是文艺复兴时代流传最广的法语歌曲之一，题材来自《圣经》次经《但以理书》中苏珊娜被两名长老调戏的故事。拉索于1560年前后创作了这首五声部版本，以当时已广为流传的同名旋律为素材，加以多声部复调的精致处理，使其成为这个题材所有改编版本中公认最出色的一首。仅凭这首一首作品的知名度，拉索便可在16世纪的法语香颂史上占有一席之地。

在16世纪，「苏珊娜」故事以多种艺术形式流传，至少有几十位作曲家为同一文本谱曲，但拉索的版本最受好评——它将法语歌词的自然音调与精炼的复调织体融为一体，同时在音乐上生动地刻画了苏珊娜的美丽与无辜、长老的邪恶贪念以及故事最后的峰回路转。这种以音乐叙事的能力，是拉索区别于他人的特质之一。

这首香颂在整个16至17世纪被反复改编为各种器乐版本，从琉特琴到管风琴，从键盘曲到吉他，不同形式的版本层出不穷。它代表了拉索在法语香颂领域的最高成就，也是他作为多语言作曲家能在法国传统中写出地道精品的最好证明。
famousScores.detailEn:
Susanne un jour ('Susanna one day') was one of the most widely disseminated French songs of the Renaissance, based on the biblical Apocrypha story of Susanna and the Elders from the Book of Daniel. Lasso composed his five-voice setting around 1560, using a melody that was already well known in France and elaborating it into one of the most refined polyphonic versions ever produced for this text. On the basis of this single work alone, Lasso would hold a significant place in the history of the sixteenth-century chanson.

At least several dozen composers set the Susanna story in the sixteenth century, but Lasso's version was consistently regarded as the finest — it integrated the natural speech rhythms of the French text with polished counterpoint while vividly characterizing Susanna's beauty and innocence, the elders' malicious desire, and the story's dramatic reversal. This ability to narrate in music was one of Lasso's most distinctive qualities.

The chanson was transcribed into dozens of instrumental versions throughout the sixteenth and seventeenth centuries — for lute, organ, keyboard, guitar, and various consort instruments — a testament to its irresistible appeal across genres. It represents Lasso's finest achievement in the French chanson repertoire and his most convincing demonstration that, despite his Flemish birth and German career, he could write in the French tradition with complete authenticity and brilliance.

famousScores.name: 《我心极其悲伤》 Tristis est anima mea
famousScores.desc: 圣周经文歌，描绘耶稣在客西马尼园的悲恸，六声部，文字绘画法之极致
famousScores.descEn: A Holy Week motet depicting Christ's anguish in Gethsemane — six voices of extraordinary expressive power and word painting
famousScores.detail:
《我心极其悲伤》是拉索最著名的圣周（Holy Week）经文歌之一，以耶稣在客西马尼园祈祷时说的话为词：「我心里甚是忧伤，几乎要死……」这首六声部作品以其深沉的悲剧性、大胆的半音进行和极具戏剧感的和声转向而著称，被誉为16世纪宗教音乐中最能表现基督人性一面的作品之一。

拉索在这首作品中展示了他作为文字绘画大师的全部才能：「悲伤」（tristis）这个词第一次出现时，音乐立刻下沉，和声变暗；当耶稣说「守在这里，与我一起醒寤」时，四个内声部形成亲密的低语；而当他说「我的时候到了」时，音乐突然宏大起来，仿佛命运之门轰然敞开。每一个词都有其音乐的肉身，令人屏息。

《我心极其悲伤》是复活节前圣周礼仪音乐的重要组成部分，至今仍在全球各地的天主教教堂和古乐合奏团中定期演唱。它与帕莱斯特里纳的《圣母悼歌》并列，被视为文艺复兴宗教音乐中描写基督受难最具深度的杰作之一，证明了16世纪的复调音乐并不只是技术展示，而是真正的精神体验。
famousScores.detailEn:
Tristis est anima mea ('My soul is sorrowful') is one of Lasso's most celebrated Holy Week motets, setting Christ's words in the Garden of Gethsemane: 'My soul is exceedingly sorrowful, even to death.' This six-voice work is renowned for its deep tragic gravity, bold chromatic voice-leading, and dramatically charged harmonic shifts; it stands among the sixteenth century's most searching portrayals of Christ's human suffering.

Lasso's word-painting mastery is on full display: when the word 'tristis' (sorrowful) first appears, the music immediately sinks and darkens; when Christ asks his disciples to 'tarry here and watch,' the inner voices murmur in hushed intimacy; when he announces 'my hour is at hand,' the texture suddenly swells as if the gates of fate are opening. Every word finds its precise musical embodiment, and the effect is breathtaking.

Tristis est anima mea remains a regular part of Holy Week liturgy in Catholic churches worldwide and a staple of early music ensembles. Standing alongside Palestrina's Stabat Mater as one of the Renaissance's most profound settings of the Passion narrative, it demonstrates that sixteenth-century polyphony could be not merely technical display but genuine spiritual experience — music that makes the distance of five centuries feel irrelevant.

famousScores.name: 《在最后的时刻》 In hora ultima
famousScores.desc: 拉索晚年深沉的冥思之作，充满对死亡的坦然与接受，带有强烈的自传性质
famousScores.descEn: A deeply inward late motet meditating on the final hour of death, with an unmistakably autobiographical character
famousScores.detail:
《在最后的时刻》是拉索晚年最深沉的作品之一，一首描写临终时刻的经文歌，充满了对死亡的沉思与接受。拉索在生命的最后十年遭受了严重的抑郁症（时人称之为「忧郁症」）的折磨，他的朋友和同事记录了他情绪崩溃、无法工作的种种经历。这首作品因此具有一种特殊的自传性质：它不是关于死亡的抽象冥想，而是一个亲历死亡阴影的人向最后审判的坦然交代。

在音乐语言上，《在最后的时刻》展示了拉索晚期特有的极度内敛风格：缓慢的节奏、低沉的音域、极简的对位，以及大量的停顿与沉默——这些沉默在音乐中说话，比音符更有力量。和声进行常常以意想不到的方向转向，仿佛心跳的不规则，仿佛生命力的逐渐减弱。

这首作品与他的绝笔《圣彼得的泪水》共同构成了拉索晚年「死亡沉思」系列的核心，是文艺复兴时代最诚实面对死亡的声乐作品之一。它的存在提醒我们：在那个充满活力和辉煌的文艺复兴盛期，也有人以音乐凝视着深渊，并在凝视中找到了某种平静。
famousScores.detailEn:
In hora ultima ('In the final hour') is one of the most inward and searching works of Lasso's late years — a motet meditating on the moment of death with a frankness born of personal experience. In his final decade, Lasso suffered from a severe melancholy (described by contemporaries as a form of madness), with friends and colleagues documenting episodes of emotional collapse and inability to work. The piece carries an unmistakably autobiographical quality: it is not an abstract meditation on mortality but the honest reckoning of a man who has lived in death's shadow.

Musically, In hora ultima shows the extreme restraint of Lasso's late style: slow tempos, a low and dark vocal register, spare counterpoint, and abundant silences — silences that speak more powerfully than the notes surrounding them. The harmonic progressions turn in unexpected directions, suggesting an irregular heartbeat, the gradual failing of vital energy, the soul preparing to depart.

Together with his final masterpiece Lagrime di San Pietro, this motet forms the core of Lasso's late 'death meditation' cycle — among the most unflinchingly honest confrontations with mortality in all Renaissance vocal music. Its existence reminds us that even at the height of the Renaissance's vitality and splendor, there were artists who gazed steadily into the abyss and found, in that gaze, a kind of austere peace.

famousScores.name: 《眼所未见》 Oculus non vidit
famousScores.desc: 六声部经文歌，以哥林多前书神秘诗句为词，和声丰富而深邃，展示拉索内省宁静的一面
famousScores.descEn: A six-voice motet on St. Paul's mystery verse, radiant in harmony and revealing Lasso's capacity for serene spiritual grandeur
famousScores.detail:
《眼所未见》是拉索根据《哥林多前书》第二章第九节创作的经文歌，歌词为：「眼所未见，耳所未闻，人心所未想到的，都是神为爱他的人所预备的。」这一神学主题——超越人类感知的神圣奥秘——在拉索的音乐中找到了完美的对应：这首六声部经文歌以丰富的和声色彩和精妙的复调织体，描绘了那个超越言语与感知的神圣境界。

这首作品以其和声的丰富性和声部写作的精妙令人叹服。拉索在这里展示的不是情感的戏剧性，而是一种宏大的宁静——六个声部如同六道光线汇聚成一片光辉，描绘着那个尚不可见的天国之美。这种以复调织体营造精神意境的手法，与帕莱斯特里纳追求的音乐纯净性有某种深层的共鸣，证明拉索并非只会戏剧化的情感表达，同样也能写出平静深邃的精神景观。

这首经文歌在16至17世纪被广泛收录于各种神圣音乐选集中，证明了它在当时的高度知名度。在今天，它以与众不同的宁静气质，成为拉索全部作品中一个特殊的精神高地，提示我们在这位情感复杂的大师内心，也有一个向往永恒平静的灵魂。
famousScores.detailEn:
Oculus non vidit ('Eye has not seen') sets words from St. Paul's First Letter to the Corinthians (2:9): 'Eye has not seen, nor ear heard, nor have entered into the heart of man the things which God has prepared for those who love him.' This theological theme — divine mystery beyond the reach of all human perception — finds a perfect musical counterpart in Lasso's setting: a six-voice motet of rich harmonic color and subtle polyphonic weaving that evokes a realm beyond words and senses.

Rather than dramatic emotion, Lasso here pursues a grandeur of spiritual calm: six voices converge like rays of light into a single radiance, painting the invisible beauty of a heaven not yet seen. This approach — using polyphonic texture to create spiritual atmosphere — resonates with Palestrina's ideals of musical purity, demonstrating that Lasso was capable of profound serenity as well as his more characteristic dramatic intensity.

The motet was widely included in sacred anthologies throughout the sixteenth and seventeenth centuries, attesting to its high contemporary reputation. Today it stands as a spiritual high point within Lasso's entire output — a reminder that within this emotionally complex and multifaceted master there also dwelt a soul capable of reaching toward eternal stillness, and that such stillness, when it came, was expressed in music of the purest radiance.

famousScores.name: 《向上帝欢呼》 Jubilate Deo
famousScores.desc: 八声部双合唱团欢庆经文歌，展示拉索驾驭宏大声乐力量的辉煌风格
famousScores.descEn: A festive eight-voice double-choir motet — a supreme example of Lasso's magnificent style in large-scale sacred choral writing
famousScores.detail:
《向上帝欢呼》是拉索为八声部双合唱团创作的欢庆性经文歌，以诗篇第100篇为词：「普天下当向耶和华欢呼！要乐意侍奉耶和华，当来向他歌唱！」这首作品集中展示了拉索在宏大声乐形式上的驾驭能力——两个四声部合唱团相互呼应、交织融合，制造出壮观的音响效果，是威尼斯分合唱传统与慕尼黑宫廷辉煌气派完美结合的产物。

拉索在巴伐利亚公爵宫廷拥有一支在当时属于欧洲顶级的音乐机构。公爵对音乐的慷慨赞助使拉索能够为数量可观的高水平歌手和器乐手创作，像《向上帝欢呼》这样的八声部作品，正是在这种得天独厚的条件下诞生的。据记载，每逢宫廷重要庆典，这类宏大的合唱作品都会震撼全场，令来访的各国使节叹为观止。

《向上帝欢呼》代表了拉索「辉煌风格」（Stile magnifico）创作的典范，是他区别于帕莱斯特里纳内敛风格的最鲜明体现之一。这种宏大合唱的传统通过威尼斯乐派的乔瓦尼·加布里埃利进一步发展，最终在巴赫的管风琴前奏曲和亨德尔的清唱剧合唱段落中达到了新的高峰，影响了整个西方宗教合唱音乐的发展方向。
famousScores.detailEn:
Jubilate Deo ('Shout joyfully to God') is a festive eight-voice motet for double choir, setting Psalm 100: 'Make a joyful noise to God, all the earth; sing praises to his name.' The piece showcases Lasso's command of the grandest vocal forces: two four-voice choirs answer, interweave, and merge to create a spectacular sonic architecture that blends the Venetian divided-choir tradition with the regal splendor of the Munich court.

At the Bavarian ducal court, Lasso presided over one of the finest musical establishments in Europe. Duke Albrecht V's generous patronage gave Lasso access to a large body of highly skilled singers and instrumentalists, and works like Jubilate Deo were born from this exceptional musical environment. Contemporary accounts describe how such grand choral pieces could electrify important state occasions, leaving visiting ambassadors and dignitaries speechless with admiration.

Jubilate Deo exemplifies what might be called Lasso's 'magnificent style' — a mode of expression quite distinct from the introspective refinement of Palestrina and one of Lasso's most distinctive contributions to the sacred choral tradition. This tradition of grand choral splendor was further developed by Giovanni Gabrieli in Venice and ultimately reached new heights in Bach's organ preludes and Handel's oratorio choruses, making Lasso's eight-voice pieces important ancestors of the Western choral tradition's most celebratory branch.

influence: 拉索的文字绘画技法直接影响了巴洛克时期的音乐修辞学传统，为蒙特威尔第等人的「第二实践」铺平了道路。他的多语言、多体裁创作展示了一种泛欧洲的音乐视野，预示了后来国际化音乐风格的到来。他晚年的《泪涌如泉》以其深沉的表现力被视为连接文艺复兴与巴洛克情感美学的桥梁之作。
influenceEn: Lasso's word-painting techniques directly shaped the Baroque tradition of musical rhetoric, paving the way for Monteverdi and the seconda pratica. His multilingual, multi-genre output embodied a pan-European musical vision that foreshadowed the international styles to come. His late masterpiece Lagrime di San Pietro, with its profoundly expressive power, is widely regarded as a bridge between Renaissance and Baroque emotional aesthetics.
